source: Petitions. Che Saffiah binti Laxamana Mohamed Amin__CO273:660:2
filename: Petitions. Che Saffiah binti Laxamana Mohamed Amin__CO273:660:2.txt
summary:
- Petition file (Colonial Office, 1940) concerning **Che Saffiah binti Laxamana Mohamed Amin**, reiterating a grievance previously handled on a **1939 file**.  
- The petition was **addressed to the Chairman of the Parliamentary/Party Committee on Colonial Affairs** and was forwarded/handled through Colonial Office channels; officials note there was **no current holder** of that chairmanship and treat it as correspondence.  
- **Proposed decision/response:** a draft reply (to **Rt. Hon. C.R. Attlee, M.P.**) states the Secretary of State sees **no reason to intervene** after reviewing reports from the **High Commissioner for the Malay States**.  
- **Core issue:** Che Saffiah sought continuation of a **compassionate allowance/pension**; the allowance had been granted in **1904** to her family with conditions that it would end when sons reached **21** and when the daughter **married**.  
- **Background cited:** she is described as daughter of a **Perak Malay Chief**, a signatory of the **Treaty of Pangkor**, and among Perak chiefs who accompanied **ex-Sultan Abdullah** into exile to the **Seychelles** after the murder of the first British Resident (date not stated here).  
- **Key event affecting entitlement:** Che Saffiah **married a Johore Government Officer** (who died **4 years** before the 1940 correspondence), and her allowance **ceased upon marriage** per the original conditions; earlier petitions to the High Commissioner’s predecessors were **not reopened**.  
- **Government stance:** the **Government of Perak** is said to be **firmly opposed** to granting any pension “however small,” fearing it would prompt **similar claims**; a brother’s request for a political pension was also declined.  
- **Notable dates/handling:** internal minutes and routing span **12 March–3 May 1940** (including acknowledgments and forwarding to **Sir E. Breweston**); parts of the file are marked **“Destroyed under Statute”** (3 May 1940 noted).
